brief.name: Lindy
brief.what_they_do: Lindy is an AI executive assistant that handles administrative work like email triage, meeting scheduling, and follow-ups via iMessage, SMS, and email integrations. It learns user communication style and automates inbox management, calendar coordination, and meeting prep.
key_features: Privacy-first with GDPR, HIPAA, AICPA, PIPEDA compliance

signals.detail: Product prominently features 'Chat via iMessage / SMS' as core feature in Plus tier pricing; iMessage shown in hero demo with tasks like 'Clear my inbox · Prep for my 2pm · Reply to Mark · Book a flight to SF'
Pricing tier explicitly lists 'Chat via iMessage / SMS' as primary interface; demo shows conversational task execution through iMessage
Customer logos include Shopify, Apple, Adobe, McKinsey, NVIDIA, Harvard, Airbnb, Stanford, Autodesk, AppLovin
Enterprise tier includes 'HIPAA Compliant and signed BAA'; security section lists GDPR, HIPAA, AICPA, PIPEDA compliance
Product connects with 'hundreds of integrations' and can 'read your Slack, cross-reference your calendar, and draft a response in Gmail'
Plus tier at $49.99/month, Pro tier at $59.99/month with annual discount; Enterprise custom pricing

brief.brief: Lindy is an AI executive assistant that automates administrative work (email, meetings, follow-ups) and communicates natively via iMessage and SMS alongside email and Slack integrations. The product is used by enterprise customers including Apple, Shopify, and McKinsey, with HIPAA compliance and privacy-first architecture. Lindy's messaging-first interface and agentic notification model position it as a next-generation alternative to human assistants and traditional scheduling tools.

event.story:
THE GOAL:
Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that replaces the human assistant entirely — handling email triage, meeting prep, follow-ups, and calendar chaos through a conversational interface that executives already live in: iMessage. Their site hero shows a user typing 'Clear my inbox · Prep for my 2pm · Reply to Mark · Book a flight to SF' directly in iMessage. The bet is that the highest-trust channel becomes the command center for work. [imessage_native]

THE OBSTACLE:
That bet only holds if iMessage delivery is airtight. Lindy's enterprise customers — Shopify, Apple, McKinsey, NVIDIA — are running mission-critical workflows through this channel [enterprise_adoption]. A missed reminder before a board meeting, a dropped follow-up to a partner, a throttled message during an Apple infrastructure event: any one of these turns 'AI assistant' into 'liability.' iMessage is also the only channel here that Lindy does not fully control — Apple's delivery layer is opaque, and at enterprise scale, that opacity compounds. [imessage_native, compliance_ready]

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
Building on raw iMessage infrastructure means Lindy's team is absorbing Apple's throttling behavior, debugging delivery silently, and shipping without tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, or voice messages — the signals that make a conversation feel alive rather than asynchronous [imessage_native]. At HIPAA-compliant enterprise scale [compliance_ready], every undelivered message is also an audit event. The current approach trades feature depth and resilience for speed-to-market — which works until an Apple throttling event hits a McKinsey partner mid-deal.

THE BETTER PATH:
Photon's imessage-kit is an open-source TS SDK with 20K+ developers and ~2,300 GitHub stars [seller_pack]. The Advanced tier adds tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, voice messages, edit/recall — the layer that makes Lindy feel like a real conversation partner, not a bot [seller_pack]. Ditto ran 42,000+ users across five campuses with 99.87% message delivery and zero downtime during Apple throttling events on this infrastructure [seller_pack]. For a HIPAA-compliant product serving Apple and McKinsey, that delivery record matters more than any feature list. Spectrum (photon.codes/spectrum) also opens a single-SDK path to Telegram and Discord if Lindy ever wants to extend the command-center model beyond iMessage [seller_pack].

hey [Name] - Lindy's iMessage-first bet is the right one — curious if delivery resilience at enterprise scale has become a real engineering problem yet, github.com/photon-hq/imessage-kit

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lindy's iMessage channel survives Apple throttling events without downtime, ships typing indicators and read receipts that make the assistant feel present rather than delayed, and enters enterprise security reviews with auditable delivery infrastructure. The executives at Shopify and McKinsey stop wondering if their AI assistant actually sent the message — they see it.

event.story:
THE GOAL:
Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that actually replaces a human EA — not just scheduling meetings, but proactively clearing inboxes, prepping for calls, and sending follow-ups through the channels executives already live in. Their site hero says it plainly: 'Clear my inbox · Prep for my 2pm · Reply to Mark · Book a flight to SF' — all triggered through iMessage [imessage_native].

THE OBSTACLE:
iMessage is not a notification channel for Lindy — it is the primary interface. Their Plus tier lists 'Chat via iMessage / SMS' as a core product feature, not a nice-to-have [messaging_first_ux]. That means when iMessage hiccups — throttling, delivery failures, infrastructure gaps — the assistant goes silent at exactly the wrong moment. An EA that doesn't respond isn't an EA; it's a liability. And Lindy's enterprise customer base [enterprise_adoption] expects the reliability of a human, not the fragility of a bolted-on messaging hack.

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
There is no public signal that Lindy has invested in hardened iMessage infrastructure. Their tech stack is undisclosed [tech_stack: empty]. The product's iMessage integration is presented as a feature, not as an infrastructure layer with delivery guarantees, tapbacks, typing indicators, or read receipts [imessage_native]. At $49.99–$59.99/month per user with enterprise contracts layered on top [pricing_transparency], a message that doesn't arrive — or arrives without the conversational fidelity of a real iMessage thread — erodes the core value proposition. Basic send-and-receive is not enough when the product promise is 'your AI EA.'

THE BETTER PATH:
Photon's imessage-kit is open-source TypeScript infrastructure — 20K+ developers, ~2,300 GitHub stars — built specifically for agents that need to reach users through iMessage reliably [seller_pack]. The Advanced iMessage Kit adds tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, voice messages, and edit/recall: the signals that make a conversation feel like a real EA, not a chatbot [seller_pack]. Ditto runs 42,000+ users across five campuses on this infrastructure with 99.87% message delivery and zero downtime during Apple throttling events [seller_pack]. TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the same SDK ($47M Series A, ICONIQ Growth) [seller_pack]. Lindy's agents already reach users through iMessage — Photon makes that channel durable enough to stake an enterprise contract on.

hey [Name] - iMessage as Lindy's primary interface is a sharp call — github.com/photon-hq/imessage-kit is what we built so agents don't go silent when Apple throttles.

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lindy ships iMessage infrastructure that matches the reliability promise their enterprise pricing demands — delivery guarantees, conversational fidelity (typing indicators, tapbacks, read receipts), and a multi-channel fallback via Spectrum if iMessage is ever unavailable [seller_pack]. Their agents stop being dependent on a fragile messaging layer and start being as dependable as the human EAs they are replacing. That is a product story worth telling to the next enterprise logo on their list.

event.story:
THE GOAL:
Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that replaces human admins — not just for scheduling, but for real-time task execution via the channels professionals already live in. Their site shows the vision plainly: a user texts 'Clear my inbox · Prep for my 2pm · Reply to Mark · Book a flight to SF' over iMessage and Lindy just handles it [imessage_native]. Enterprise customers at the level of Shopify, Adobe, and McKinsey logos on their homepage [enterprise_adoption] expect that interface to be bulletproof.

THE OBSTACLE:
Lindy's value proposition lives or dies on message delivery. When an executive texts Lindy 'prep for my 2pm' at 1:45pm, a dropped or delayed message isn't an inconvenience — it's a product failure. iMessage is Apple-controlled infrastructure: throttling events, rate limits, and delivery gaps are real operational risks that Lindy's current iMessage integration must absorb silently [imessage_native]. There's no public signal that Lindy has hardened this layer, and at the enterprise tier — HIPAA-signed BAAs, Fortune 500 logos — the tolerance for messaging infrastructure failure is effectively zero [compliance_ready].

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
Building on top of iMessage without purpose-built infrastructure means Lindy is likely absorbing Apple's platform constraints directly. The 'Chat via iMessage / SMS' feature is listed as a core differentiator in their Plus pricing tier [messaging_first_ux] — which means it's a selling point, not a fallback. If iMessage delivery degrades during an Apple infrastructure event, Lindy has no documented multi-channel fallback layer to keep the agent loop alive. At $49.99–$59.99/month per user with enterprise custom tiers [pricing_transparency], customers are paying for reliability that the current stack may not be architected to guarantee.

THE BETTER PATH:
Photon's imessage-kit is open-source TypeScript infrastructure built specifically for AI agents operating over iMessage — 20K+ developers, ~2,300 GitHub stars on github.com/photon-hq/imessage-kit [seller_pack]. The Advanced tier adds tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, voice messages, and edit/recall — the native iMessage behaviors that make an AI assistant feel like a real conversation partner, not a bot [seller_pack]. Spectrum (photon.codes/spectrum) adds a single-SDK fallback layer across iMessage, Telegram, and Discord, so if Apple throttles, Lindy's agent loop keeps running on the next available channel [seller_pack]. TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on this same SDK after a $47M Series A from ICONIQ Growth [seller_pack].
The angle: your iMessage interface is your product — we built the infrastructure layer that keeps it alive when Apple doesn't cooperate.

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lindy's iMessage channel becomes a hardened delivery layer, not a dependency risk. Typing indicators and read receipts make the assistant feel present and responsive rather than silent [seller_pack]. If Apple throttles, Spectrum keeps the agent loop running across channels without the user noticing a gap [seller_pack]. For enterprise customers on HIPAA-signed plans [compliance_ready], that delivery guarantee is the difference between a productivity tool and a trusted system of record. Lindy ships faster on the channel that defines their product, without owning the infrastructure complexity underneath it.
